text: Developing a mobile app requires careful planning. First, you must choose between native and cross-platform development. Native apps, built specifically for iOS or Android, often offer better performance and access to device features. Cross-platform tools like React Native allow you to write one codebase for both systems, saving time and cost. A crucial element is responsive design, ensuring your app’s layout adapts smoothly to different screen sizes. Once built, you will submit your app to official app stores, like Google Play or the Apple App Store, which review it for quality and security. Performance is key—users expect fast loading and smooth interactions. Poorly optimized apps often get negative reviews and low ratings, hurting success.
